title: Structural Fire (Victim / Inside)
keywords: I am trapped in a fire
steps: Get out immediately and stay out. Do not stop for belongings.
If there is smoke, stay low to the floor and crawl to the nearest exit.
Touch doors with the back of your hand before opening; if hot, find another way.
If trapped: Close all doors, seal cracks with cloth/tape, and signal from a window.
Call 112/911 once you are outside or if trapped inside.

title: Structural Fire (Witness / Outside)
steps: Call 112/911 immediately. Provide the exact address.
Keep a safe distance from the building.
Inform emergency services if you believe people are trapped inside.
Do NOT enter the building under any circumstances.
Wait for firefighters and direct them to the fire source if known.

title: Electrical Fire (Victim / Inside)
steps: Do NOT use water. Turn off the main power switch immediately if safe to reach.
Use a Class C or CO2 fire extinguisher if the fire is small.
If the fire is inside walls or spreading, evacuate the building immediately.
Call 112/911 and specify it is an electrical fire.
Do not re-enter or turn power back on until an electrician inspects the damage.

title: Electrical Fire (Witness / Outside)
keywords: I see sparks on power line
steps: Call 112/911 and the electric utility company.
Keep at least 10 meters (33 feet) away from the sparks or downed lines.
Warn others to stay back.
Do NOT touch any metal fences or objects near the electrical source.
Wait for professionals to arrive.

title: Wildfire / Forest Fire (Victim / Exposed)
keywords: I am trapped by a bushfire
fire in the mountains near me
steps: Evacuate immediately if ordered. Do not wait for a second warning.
Close all windows/doors and turn off AC to prevent embers from entering.
Wear long-sleeved natural fibers (cotton/wool) to protect from heat.
If trapped, find a clear area (rocky ground or water) and stay low.
Call 112/911 with your exact location if you cannot evacuate.

title: Wildfire / Forest Fire (Witness / Reporting)
keywords: I see smoke in the forest
steps: Call 112/911. Describe the location and size of the smoke column.
Move away from the direction of the wind (upwind).
Stay informed via local radio or emergency apps.
Do NOT fly drones near the area (interferes with firefighting aircraft).
Alert neighbors if you are in a rural area.

title: Chimney Fire
steps: Evacuate everyone from the house immediately.
Call emergency services.
If safe, close the fireplace doors or dampen the air intake to starve the fire of oxygen.
Do NOT throw water down the chimney (can cause explosive steam or crack the flue).
Wait outside for the fire department to arrive.
Do not use the fireplace again until inspected and cleaned by a professional.

title: Wildfire Survival in Vehicle
steps: If you cannot escape, park the car away from heavy vegetation.
Turn on the headlights and hazard lights to stay visible.
Close all windows and vents. Turn the AC to 'recirculate'.
Get on the floor of the car and cover yourself with a wool or cotton blanket/coat.
Stay in the car until the main fire front passes; the car's body provides protection against radiant heat.
Expect the car to shake and fill with light smoke. Stay low and breathe through a damp cloth.

title: Kitchen Grease Fire (Victim / Inside)
steps: Do NOT throw water on a grease fire. It will cause an explosion.
If safe, turn off the heat source immediately.
Cover the pan with a metal lid or cookie sheet to smother the flames.
Leave it covered until completely cool. Do not move the pan.
If the fire spreads to cabinets, evacuate and call 112/911.

title: Kitchen Grease Fire (Witness / Responder)
keywords: I see fire in a restaurant kitchen
steps: Call 112/911 immediately.
Do NOT enter a smoke-filled kitchen.
If you are with the victim, ensure they do not throw water on the fire.
Have everyone evacuate the area.
Inform arriving firefighters of the fire's exact location.

title: Clothing Fire (Stop, Drop, Roll)
steps: STOP immediately. Do not run, as running fans the flames and makes the fire grow.
DROP to the ground and cover your face with your hands.
ROLL back and forth until the fire is out.
If someone else is on fire, smother the flames with a heavy blanket or coat.
Once the fire is out, cool the burned area with cool (not cold) water for 10-20 minutes.
Do not apply ice, ointments, or bandages to a severe burn; seek medical help.

title: Office / Workplace Fire
steps: Activate the nearest fire alarm pull station immediately.
Evacuate using the nearest safe exit or stairs. Never use elevators.
If you are the last one out, close doors behind you to contain the fire.
Proceed to the designated assembly area outside.
If trapped, stay in a room with a window and call 112/911. Signal your location through the window.
Account for all colleagues and report missing persons to the fire department.

title: Boat / Vessel Fire
steps: Alert everyone on board and ensure all passengers have life jackets on.
Stop the boat or position it so the wind blows the fire away from the vessel.
If the fire is in the engine space, shut off fuel valves and use the fire port to discharge an extinguisher.
Radio for help (Mayday or Pan-Pan) with your exact coordinates.
Prepare to abandon ship if the fire cannot be controlled.
If you must enter the water, stay together and stay away from any burning fuel on the surface.

title: Vehicle Battery Fire - EV (Victim / Driver)
keywords: I smell chemicals in my Tesla
my electric vehicle is on fire
steps: Pull over immediately and turn off the vehicle.
Evacuate all passengers and move at least 50 meters (165 feet) upwind.
Do NOT inhale the smoke; EV battery smoke is highly toxic (fluorides).
Call 112/911 and specify: 'Electric Vehicle battery fire'.
Do not attempt to use a standard extinguisher on the battery.

title: Vehicle Battery Fire - EV (Witness / Responder)
keywords: I see an electric car on fire
smoke from under an electric car
steps: Call 112/911 immediately. Specify it is an Electric Vehicle.
Stay at least 50 meters away. EV batteries can explode or vent violently.
Warn others to stay upwind to avoid toxic fumes.
Do NOT approach the vehicle; 'thermal runaway' can cause re-ignition.
Inform responders if you saw the fire start in the battery/underbody.

title: High-Rise Building Fire
steps: Check the stairwell for smoke before entering; if it is smoke-filled, return to your room.
Never use elevators. They can become traps and may open on the fire floor.
If you cannot evacuate, stay in your room, seal the door with wet towels, and call emergency services.
Signal your location by hanging a brightly colored cloth or using a flashlight at the window.
If there is smoke in the room, stay low to the floor where the air is fresher.
Do NOT break windows unless absolutely necessary (broken glass can injure people below and provide oxygen to the fire).

title: Gas Station Fire (Victim / Refueling)
keywords: I am refueling and there is a fire
I am at a gas station fire
steps: Do NOT remove the nozzle from the car if it is on fire. This sprays flames.
Immediately hit the emergency shut-off button at the pump or station.
Evacuate everyone from the vehicle and move at least 100 meters away.
Call 112/911 once you are at a safe distance.
Warn other drivers near the pumps to evacuate immediately.

title: Gas Station Fire (Witness / Observer)
keywords: I see a fire at a gas station
steps: Call 112/911 immediately.
Do NOT drive toward or enter the gas station area.
Stay at least 100 meters (330 feet) away from the facility.
Warn others to stay back; fuel fires can spread to underground tanks.
Wait for professional firefighters.

title: Chemical / Industrial Fire (Victim / Exposed)
keywords: I am exposed to a chemical fire
I am in a HAZMAT fire
steps: Evacuate immediately in an upwind direction (wind at your back).
Call 112/911. State: 'Chemical fire exposure'.
If chemicals touched your skin, rinse with water for 20 minutes.
Move to higher ground if the gas is heavier than air.
Stay away from the area until cleared by HAZMAT teams.

title: Chemical / Industrial Fire (Witness / Reporting)
keywords: smoke from a chemical plant witness
steps: Call 112/911 immediately. Mention it is an industrial/chemical site.
Stay at least 500 meters (1,600 feet) away from the site.
Warn others to stay upwind to avoid toxic inhalation.
Do NOT attempt to help victims inside without specialized gear.
Report any placards or UN numbers visible on the building/trucks.

title: Vehicle Fire - ICE (Victim / Driver)
keywords: I am in a burning vehicle
steps: Pull over and turn off the engine immediately.
Get everyone out and move 30 meters (100 feet) away.
Do NOT open the hood. Air will fuel the fire.
Do not return to the vehicle for any items.

title: Vehicle Fire - ICE (Witness / Responder)
keywords: I see a car on fire
smoke from a vehicle on the road
steps: Call 112/911 immediately.
Pull over at a safe distance and turn on your hazards.
Do NOT approach the burning vehicle; fuel tanks can explode.
Warn other drivers and keep traffic moving away from the fire.
Inform responders if people are still inside the vehicle.

title: Tunnel / Underground Fire (Victim / Trapped)
keywords: I am trapped in a tunnel fire
my car is on fire in a tunnel
steps: Stop your vehicle, turn off the engine, and leave the keys in the ignition.
Evacuate immediately via the nearest emergency exit. Do NOT stay in your car.
Stay low to the ground to avoid smoke inhalation.
Follow floor-level lighting or exit signs.
Use an emergency alcove phone if you cannot find an exit.

title: Tunnel / Underground Fire (Witness / Reporting)
keywords: smoke from a metro station witness
steps: Call 112/911 immediately.
Do NOT enter the tunnel if you see smoke.
Stop before the entrance and warn other drivers to stop.
Listen for emergency broadcasts on your radio.
Wait for emergency services to arrive.

title: Metal Fire (Class D)
steps: NEVER use water, CO2, or standard foam on a metal fire; it will cause a violent explosion or release hydrogen gas.
Identify the metal involved if possible (e.g., magnesium, titanium, sodium).
Use only a specialized Class D fire extinguisher or cover with dry sand/salt to smother the fire.
Evacuate the immediate area and call emergency services, specifying it is a METAL fire.
Expect extremely high temperatures and intense, blinding white light.
Do NOT attempt to move the burning metal.

title: Fire Entrapment / Cannot Escape
steps: Stay calm. Close all doors between you and the fire to slow the spread.
Use wet towels, clothing, or tape to seal all cracks around the door and vents.
If smoke enters, open a window slightly for fresh air, but DO NOT break the glass. If smoke comes in from the outside, close it immediately.
Stay low to the floor where the air is cleaner.
Call 112/911, state exactly where you are (floor, room number).
Signal for help from the window using a brightly colored cloth or flashlight.

title: Severe Smoke Inhalation
steps: Move the person to fresh air immediately. Do NOT become a victim yourself.
Call 112/911 and report 'Smoke inhalation, need medical immediately'.
If the person is unconscious and not breathing, begin CPR (Chest compressions and rescue breaths) if trained.
If conscious, sit them up to help with breathing and loosen tight clothing around their neck or chest.
Do NOT give them anything to eat or drink.
Monitor their breathing continuously until emergency responders arrive.
